• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Analyse et Ingénierie des Données de la Chlordécone — Volet R

Younes — ENSAR A1 SD

2026-03-30

1 Contexte

La chlordécone est un pesticide organochloré utilisé aux Antilles françaises jusqu’au début des années 1990. Ce document présente l’analyse en R du dataset BaseCLD2026.csv, en parallèle du notebook Python.

2 Import et bibliothèques

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(corrplot)
library(factoextra)   # ACP, CAH
library(FactoMineR)   # ACP, AFC
library(cluster)      # K-means, silhouette
library(class)        # KNN

# Chargement
df <- read_delim("../data/BaseCLD2026.csv", delim = ";", locale = locale(encoding = "latin1"))
cat("Dimensions :", nrow(df), "×", ncol(df), "\n")
## Dimensions : 31126 × 22
glimpse(df)
## Rows: 31,126
## Columns: 22
## $ ID                    <dbl> 20143, 20143, 20143, 20143, 20143, 20143, 20143,…
## $ ANNEE                 <dbl> 2010, 2010, 2010, 2010, 2010, 2010, 2010, 2010, …
## $ COMMU_LAB             <chr> "GROS-MORNE", "GROS-MORNE", "GROS-MORNE", "GROS-…
## $ RAIN                  <chr> "2000-3000", "2000-3000", "2000-3000", "2000-300…
## $ Sol_simple            <chr> "Andosol", "Andosol", "Andosol", "Andosol", "And…
## $ type_sol              <chr> "Intergrades Sols â\u0080¦ allophane relativemen…
## $ Date_prelevement      <chr> "24/05/2007", "24/05/2007", "24/05/2007", "24/05…
## $ Date_enregistrement   <chr> "24/05/2007", "24/05/2007", "24/05/2007", "24/05…
## $ Date_analyse          <chr> "24/05/2007", "24/05/2007", "24/05/2007", "24/05…
## $ Operateur_chld        <chr> "=", "=", "=", "=", "=", "=", "=", "<", "<", "<"…
## $ Taux_Chlordecone      <dbl> 4.6000, 4.6000, 4.6000, 4.6000, 4.6000, 4.6000, …
## $ Operateur_5b          <chr> "=", "=", "=", "=", "=", "=", "=", "<", "=", "="…
## $ Taux_5b_hydro         <chr> "0,07", "0,07", "0,07", "0,07", "0,07", "0,07", …
## $ histoBanane_Histo_ban <dbl> 2, 2, 3, 1, 2, 3, 2, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 2, 1, 1, …
## $ mnt_tpi_mean          <dbl> 5.8059665, 5.6835893, 2.2394574, 4.0383730, 0.59…
## $ mnt_tri_mean          <dbl> 8.033367, 7.920563, 7.108432, 7.530090, 6.637082…
## $ mnt_rugosite_mean     <dbl> 21.593658, 20.944901, 20.085883, 23.427731, 20.1…
## $ mnt_ombrage_mean      <dbl> 131.1740, 134.6082, 139.4205, 121.6037, 134.0651…
## $ mnt_exposition_mean   <dbl> 79.44795, 76.98569, 76.06472, 92.39245, 83.93061…
## $ mnt_pente_mean        <dbl> 39.04310, 38.12367, 34.97668, 38.31580, 33.87773…
## $ X                     <dbl> 714300.8, 714303.7, 714309.4, 714294.2, 714303.8…
## $ Y                     <dbl> 1626344, 1626354, 1626360, 1626321, 1626341, 162…

3 VOLET 1 — Ingénierie des Données

3.1 Manipulation tabulaire (dplyr)

# GROUP_BY + SUMMARISE
resume_commune <- df %>%
  group_by(COMMU_LAB) %>%
  summarise(
    nb_parcelles = n(),
    taux_moyen = mean(Taux_Chlordecone, na.rm = TRUE),
    taux_median = median(Taux_Chlordecone, na.rm = TRUE),
    taux_max = max(Taux_Chlordecone, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(taux_moyen))

head(resume_commune, 10)
## # A tibble: 10 × 5
##    COMMU_LAB       nb_parcelles taux_moyen taux_median taux_max
##    <chr>                  <int>      <dbl>       <dbl>    <dbl>
##  1 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)         3725      2.80       2.76      17.4 
##  2 GRAND'RIVIERE            125      1.84       0.336     15.5 
##  3 MACOUBA                  698      1.70       1.5        5.31
##  4 BASSE-POINTE             992      1.38       0.09       9.3 
##  5 TRINITE(LA)             1101      0.684      0.06       3.6 
##  6 MARIGOT(LE)              475      0.613      0.125      2   
##  7 LORRAIN(LE)             1763      0.584      0.185     12.8 
##  8 SAINT-PIERRE             339      0.561      0.0053     4.13
##  9 GROS-MORNE              2093      0.547      0.0033     5.77
## 10 LAMENTIN(LE)            1930      0.442      0.0033     5.67
# FILTER
df_forte <- df %>%
  filter(Taux_Chlordecone > 1)
cat("Parcelles fortement contaminées (>1 mg/kg) :", nrow(df_forte), "\n")
## Parcelles fortement contaminées (>1 mg/kg) : 5560
# MUTATE
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    classe_contamination = case_when(
      Taux_Chlordecone <= 0.01 ~ "Très faible",
      Taux_Chlordecone <= 0.1  ~ "Faible",
      Taux_Chlordecone <= 1    ~ "Modéré",
      Taux_Chlordecone <= 5    ~ "Élevé",
      TRUE                      ~ "Très élevé"
    ),
    log_taux_cld = log1p(Taux_Chlordecone),
    detection = ifelse(Operateur_chld == "=", "Détecté", "Sous seuil (<)")
  )
table(df$classe_contamination)
## 
##       Élevé      Faible      Modéré  Très élevé Très faible 
##        4914        3423        5217         646       16926
# ORDER_BY (arrange)
top15 <- df %>%
  arrange(desc(Taux_Chlordecone)) %>%
  select(ID, COMMU_LAB, ANNEE, Taux_Chlordecone, Sol_simple) %>%
  head(15)
top15
## # A tibble: 15 × 5
##       ID COMMU_LAB       ANNEE Taux_Chlordecone Sol_simple
##    <dbl> <chr>           <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>     
##  1 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  2 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  3 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  4 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  5 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  6 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  7 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  8 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
##  9 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
## 10 21120 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)  2011             17.4 Andosol   
## 11 20421 GRAND'RIVIERE    2010             15.5 No data   
## 12 20421 GRAND'RIVIERE    2010             15.5 Andosol   
## 13 20421 GRAND'RIVIERE    2010             15.5 Andosol   
## 14 20421 GRAND'RIVIERE    2010             15.5 No data   
## 15 20421 GRAND'RIVIERE    2010             15.5 Andosol
# Détection d'incohérences
cat("Dates 9999 :", sum(grepl("9999", df$Date_analyse)), "\n")
## Dates 9999 : 2702
cat("Communes NA :", sum(is.na(df$COMMU_LAB)), "\n")
## Communes NA : 298
cat("Hors Martinique :", sum(df$COMMU_LAB == "Hors Martinique", na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
## Hors Martinique : 27

3.2 Recodage et transformation

# Recodage pluviométrie
df <- df %>%
  mutate(RAIN_cat = recode(RAIN,
    "0-1250" = "Faible", "1250-1500" = "Modérée",
    "1500-2000" = "Modérée-haute", "2000-3000" = "Haute",
    "3000-5000" = "Très haute", "5000-8000" = "Extrême"
  ))
table(df$RAIN_cat)
## 
##       Extrême        Faible         Haute       Modérée Modérée-haute 
##            92           312         17425          1490          7658 
##    Très haute 
##          4149
# Recodage histoBanane
df <- df %>%
  mutate(histo_banane_label = recode(as.character(histoBanane_Histo_ban),
    "1" = "Jamais banane", "2" = "Banane passée", "3" = "Banane actuelle"
  ))
table(df$histo_banane_label, useNA = "ifany")
## 
## Banane actuelle   Banane passée   Jamais banane            <NA> 
##            2076            4697            6370           17983

3.3 Jointures

# Création table auxiliaire
communes_info <- tibble(
  COMMU_LAB = c("GROS-MORNE", "DUCOS", "SAINTE-MARIE", "LORRAIN(LE)",
                "ROBERT(LE)", "COMMUNE_FICTIVE"),
  zone_geo = c("Centre", "Sud", "Nord-Atl", "Nord-Atl", "Centre-Atl", "Nord"),
  pop_2020 = c(10200, 18500, 15800, 7200, 22300, 999)
)

resume <- df %>%
  group_by(COMMU_LAB) %>%
  summarise(taux_moyen = mean(Taux_Chlordecone, na.rm = TRUE), n = n())

# left_join
lj <- left_join(resume, communes_info, by = "COMMU_LAB")
cat("LEFT JOIN :", nrow(lj), "lignes, NaN zone_geo :", sum(is.na(lj$zone_geo)), "\n")
## LEFT JOIN : 36 lignes, NaN zone_geo : 31
# right_join
rj <- right_join(resume, communes_info, by = "COMMU_LAB")
cat("RIGHT JOIN :", nrow(rj), "lignes\n")
## RIGHT JOIN : 6 lignes
# inner_join
ij <- inner_join(resume, communes_info, by = "COMMU_LAB")
cat("INNER JOIN :", nrow(ij), "lignes\n")
## INNER JOIN : 5 lignes
# full_join
fj <- full_join(resume, communes_info, by = "COMMU_LAB")
cat("FULL JOIN :", nrow(fj), "lignes\n")
## FULL JOIN : 37 lignes

3.4 Contrôle et flux

# IF / ELSE avec sapply
classifier_risque <- function(taux) {
  if (taux > 5) "Critique"
  else if (taux > 1) "Élevé"
  else if (taux > 0.1) "Modéré"
  else if (taux > 0.01) "Faible"
  else "Négligeable"
}
df$niveau_risque <- sapply(df$Taux_Chlordecone, classifier_risque)
table(df$niveau_risque)
## 
##    Critique       Élevé      Faible      Modéré Négligeable 
##         646        4914        3423        5217       16926
# SWITCH
comportement_sol <- function(sol) {
  switch(as.character(sol),
    "Andosol" = "Fort potentiel de rétention",
    "Ferralsol" = "Rétention modérée",
    "Nitisol" = "Rétention modérée-forte",
    "Vertisol" = "Adsorption variable",
    "Inconnu"
  )
}
df$comportement_sol <- sapply(df$Sol_simple, comportement_sol)
table(df$comportement_sol)
## 
##         Adsorption variable Fort potentiel de rétention 
##                        6921                       10953 
##                     Inconnu           Rétention modérée 
##                        5007                        5182 
##     Rétention modérée-forte 
##                        3063
# Boucle FOR
cat("Évolution par année :\n")
## Évolution par année :
for (annee in sort(unique(df$ANNEE))) {
  sub <- df %>% filter(ANNEE == annee)
  cat(sprintf("  %d : moy=%.4f, med=%.4f, n=%d\n",
              annee, mean(sub$Taux_Chlordecone), median(sub$Taux_Chlordecone), nrow(sub)))
}
##   2010 : moy=2.7782, med=2.6500, n=3928
##   2011 : moy=1.3894, med=0.1100, n=1163
##   2012 : moy=0.4954, med=0.0200, n=1479
##   2013 : moy=0.3682, med=0.0033, n=1562
##   2014 : moy=0.3697, med=0.0800, n=1153
##   2015 : moy=0.5204, med=0.0033, n=3158
##   2016 : moy=0.2191, med=0.0033, n=4316
##   2017 : moy=0.5291, med=0.0060, n=5141
##   2018 : moy=0.1120, med=0.0020, n=8567
##   2019 : moy=0.3805, med=0.1160, n=659
# APPLY / LAPPLY / SAPPLY
cols_num <- c("Taux_Chlordecone", "mnt_pente_mean", "mnt_rugosite_mean", "mnt_exposition_mean")
cat("apply — moyennes :\n")
## apply — moyennes :
print(sapply(df[cols_num], function(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)))
##    Taux_Chlordecone      mnt_pente_mean   mnt_rugosite_mean mnt_exposition_mean 
##           0.6676626          20.2671067          13.4440683         170.1709305
cat("\nlapply — types :\n")
## 
## lapply — types :
print(lapply(df[cols_num], class))
## $Taux_Chlordecone
## [1] "numeric"
## 
## $mnt_pente_mean
## [1] "numeric"
## 
## $mnt_rugosite_mean
## [1] "numeric"
## 
## $mnt_exposition_mean
## [1] "numeric"

3.5 Gestion des dates

# Conversion des dates avec lubridate
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Date_prel_dt = dmy(Date_prelevement),
    Date_enr_dt = dmy(Date_enregistrement),
    Date_ana_dt = dmy(Date_analyse),
    annee_prel = year(Date_prel_dt),
    mois_prel = month(Date_prel_dt),
    jour_semaine = wday(Date_prel_dt, label = TRUE),
    trimestre = quarter(Date_prel_dt)
  )

# Délai prélèvement → analyse
df$delai_jours <- as.numeric(df$Date_ana_dt - df$Date_prel_dt)
delais_valides <- df$delai_jours[df$delai_jours > 0 & df$delai_jours < 3650]
cat("Délai moyen prél→analyse :", mean(delais_valides, na.rm = TRUE), "jours\n")
## Délai moyen prél→analyse : 110.9872 jours

3.6 Valeurs manquantes

# Bilan
missing <- data.frame(
  variable = names(df),
  nb_na = sapply(df, function(x) sum(is.na(x))),
  pct_na = sapply(df, function(x) round(sum(is.na(x)) / length(x) * 100, 2))
) %>%
  filter(nb_na > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_na))

print(missing)
##                                    variable nb_na pct_na
## histoBanane_Histo_ban histoBanane_Histo_ban 17983  57.77
## histo_banane_label       histo_banane_label 17983  57.77
## type_sol                           type_sol  2609   8.38
## COMMU_LAB                         COMMU_LAB   298   0.96
## Sol_simple                       Sol_simple    74   0.24
## mnt_tpi_mean                   mnt_tpi_mean    28   0.09
## mnt_tri_mean                   mnt_tri_mean    28   0.09
## mnt_rugosite_mean         mnt_rugosite_mean    28   0.09
## mnt_ombrage_mean           mnt_ombrage_mean    28   0.09
## mnt_exposition_mean     mnt_exposition_mean    28   0.09
## mnt_pente_mean               mnt_pente_mean    28   0.09
## Taux_5b_hydro                 Taux_5b_hydro    12   0.04
# Visualisation
ggplot(missing, aes(x = reorder(variable, pct_na), y = pct_na)) +
  geom_col(fill = ifelse(missing$pct_na > 30, "red", ifelse(missing$pct_na > 10, "orange", "green"))) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Taux de valeurs manquantes", x = "", y = "% NA") +
  theme_minimal()

4 VOLET 2 — Analyse de Données

4.1 Analyse exploratoire

# Statistiques descriptives
summary(df$Taux_Chlordecone)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##  0.0010  0.0024  0.0033  0.6677  0.4100 17.3500
cat("Skewness :", moments::skewness(df$Taux_Chlordecone), "\n")
## Skewness : 3.700068
# Dashboard EDA
p1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = log1p(Taux_Chlordecone))) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution log(1+Taux)", x = "log(1+Taux)")

p2 <- ggplot(df %>% filter(!is.na(Sol_simple)),
             aes(x = Sol_simple, y = Taux_Chlordecone)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() + ylim(0, 5) +
  labs(title = "Taux par type de sol")

p3 <- df %>%
  group_by(ANNEE) %>%
  summarise(moy = mean(Taux_Chlordecone), med = median(Taux_Chlordecone)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ANNEE)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = moy, color = "Moyenne")) +
  geom_point(aes(y = moy, color = "Moyenne")) +
  geom_line(aes(y = med, color = "Médiane"), linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Évolution temporelle", y = "Taux")

library(gridExtra)
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 2)

# Corrélations
num_data <- df %>% select(Taux_Chlordecone, starts_with("mnt_")) %>% drop_na()
corrplot(cor(num_data), method = "color", type = "upper",
         tl.cex = 0.8, addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = 0.7)

# Carte spatiale
ggplot(df, aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Taux_Chlordecone)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "yellow", high = "red", limits = c(0, 5), oob = scales::squish) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Carte de contamination — Martinique", color = "Taux CLD") +
  theme_minimal()

4.2 ACP (FactoMineR)

acp_data <- df %>%
  select(Taux_Chlordecone, starts_with("mnt_")) %>%
  drop_na()

res_acp <- PCA(acp_data, graph = FALSE)

# Scree plot
fviz_eig(res_acp, addlabels = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Éboulis des valeurs propres")

# Cercle des corrélations
fviz_pca_var(res_acp, col.var = "contrib", gradient.cols = c("blue", "yellow", "red")) +
  labs(title = "Cercle des corrélations — ACP")

# Individus colorés par Sol
fviz_pca_ind(res_acp, geom = "point", col.ind = df$Sol_simple[as.numeric(rownames(acp_data))],
             alpha.ind = 0.3, pointsize = 1) +
  labs(title = "Projection des individus — ACP")

4.3 AFC (FactoMineR)

# Tableau de contingence
ct <- table(df$Sol_simple, df$classe_contamination)
print(ct)
##                      
##                       Élevé Faible Modéré Très élevé Très faible
##   Alluvium, Colluvium   180    290    516          0         618
##   Andosol              3076   1256   1833        490        4298
##   Ferralsol             331    603    672         29        3547
##   Nitisol               277    324    772         12        1678
##   No data               654    216    374        107        1266
##   Urban area             40    132     74          8         458
##   Vertisol              326    584    955          0        5056
# Test du Chi2
chi_test <- chisq.test(ct)
cat("Chi2 =", chi_test$statistic, ", p =", chi_test$p.value, "\n")
## Chi2 = 4649.731 , p = 0
# AFC
res_afc <- CA(ct, graph = FALSE)
fviz_ca_biplot(res_afc, repel = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "AFC — Sol × Classe de contamination")

4.4 Clustering

# K-Means
clust_data <- df %>%
  select(Taux_Chlordecone, mnt_pente_mean, mnt_rugosite_mean, mnt_exposition_mean) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  scale()

# Méthode du coude
fviz_nbclust(clust_data[sample(nrow(clust_data), 5000), ], kmeans,
             method = "wss", k.max = 8) +
  labs(title = "Méthode du coude")

# Silhouette
fviz_nbclust(clust_data[sample(nrow(clust_data), 5000), ], kmeans,
             method = "silhouette", k.max = 8) +
  labs(title = "Score silhouette")

# K-Means k=3
set.seed(42)
sample_viz <- sample(nrow(clust_data), 3000)
clust_sample <- clust_data[sample_viz, ]
km <- kmeans(clust_sample, centers = 3, nstart = 25)
fviz_cluster(km, data = clust_sample,
             geom = "point", pointsize = 1, alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(title = "Clusters K-Means (k=3)")

# CAH
sample_idx <- sample(nrow(clust_data), 2000)
hc <- hclust(dist(clust_data[sample_idx, ]), method = "ward.D2")
fviz_dend(hc, k = 4, cex = 0.5, rect = TRUE) +
  labs(title = "Dendrogramme — CAH (Ward)")

4.5 KNN

# Préparation
knn_data <- df %>%
  select(mnt_pente_mean, mnt_rugosite_mean, mnt_exposition_mean,
         mnt_ombrage_mean, mnt_tpi_mean, mnt_tri_mean,
         classe_contamination) %>%
  drop_na()

set.seed(42)
idx_train <- sample(nrow(knn_data), 0.7 * nrow(knn_data))
train <- knn_data[idx_train, ]
test <- knn_data[-idx_train, ]

X_train <- scale(train[, -7])
X_test <- scale(test[, -7], center = attr(scale(train[, -7]), "scaled:center"),
                scale = attr(scale(train[, -7]), "scaled:scale"))

# Recherche k optimal
accs <- sapply(1:20, function(k) {
  pred <- knn(X_train, X_test, train$classe_contamination, k = k)
  mean(pred == test$classe_contamination)
})

plot(1:20, accs, type = "b", xlab = "k", ylab = "Accuracy",
     main = "KNN — Recherche du k optimal")
best_k <- which.max(accs)
abline(v = best_k, col = "red", lty = 2)

cat("Meilleur k :", best_k, "— Accuracy :", max(accs), "\n")
## Meilleur k : 1 — Accuracy : 0.6707395

4.6 Tests statistiques

# ANOVA
anova_result <- aov(Taux_Chlordecone ~ Sol_simple, data = df)
cat("ANOVA — Taux ~ Sol :\n")
## ANOVA — Taux ~ Sol :
print(summary(anova_result))
##                Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value Pr(>F)    
## Sol_simple      6   7647  1274.4   582.4 <2e-16 ***
## Residuals   31045  67938     2.2                   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 74 observations effacées parce que manquantes
# Chi2
ct2 <- table(df$Sol_simple, df$detection)
print(chisq.test(ct2))
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  ct2
## X-squared = 2333.4, df = 6, p-value < 2.2e-16
# Kruskal-Wallis
cat("\nKruskal-Wallis — Taux ~ Pluviométrie :\n")
## 
## Kruskal-Wallis — Taux ~ Pluviométrie :
print(kruskal.test(Taux_Chlordecone ~ RAIN, data = df))
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Taux_Chlordecone by RAIN
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 3252.8, df = 5, p-value < 2.2e-16
# Shapiro-Wilk
cat("\nShapiro-Wilk (n=1000) :\n")
## 
## Shapiro-Wilk (n=1000) :
print(shapiro.test(sample(df$Taux_Chlordecone, 1000)))
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  sample(df$Taux_Chlordecone, 1000)
## W = 0.454, p-value < 2.2e-16
# Corrélation de Spearman
cor_test <- cor.test(df$Taux_Chlordecone, df$mnt_pente_mean,
                     method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
cat("\nSpearman — Taux vs Pente : rho =", cor_test$estimate, ", p =", cor_test$p.value, "\n")
## 
## Spearman — Taux vs Pente : rho = -0.04863729 , p = 9.345108e-18

5 Synthèse — Aide à la décision

# Profils territoriaux
profil <- df %>%
  group_by(COMMU_LAB) %>%
  summarise(
    taux_moyen = mean(Taux_Chlordecone, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_forte = mean(Taux_Chlordecone > 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    nb_mesures = n(),
    sol_dominant = names(sort(table(Sol_simple), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(taux_moyen))

cat("🚨 Zones critiques (>20% forte contamination) :\n")
## 🚨 Zones critiques (>20% forte contamination) :
critiques <- profil %>% filter(pct_forte > 20)
print(critiques)
## # A tibble: 6 × 5
##   COMMU_LAB       taux_moyen pct_forte nb_mesures sol_dominant
##   <chr>                <dbl>     <dbl>      <int> <chr>       
## 1 MORNE-ROUGE(LE)      2.80       63.3       3725 Andosol     
## 2 GRAND'RIVIERE        1.84       36.8        125 No data     
## 3 MACOUBA              1.70       65.0        698 Andosol     
## 4 BASSE-POINTE         1.38       33.8        992 Andosol     
## 5 TRINITE(LA)          0.684      28.6       1101 Andosol     
## 6 MARIGOT(LE)          0.613      28.4        475 Nitisol
cat("
═══════════════════════════════════════════
RECOMMANDATIONS — AIDE À LA DÉCISION
═══════════════════════════════════════════

1. Les Andosols et Nitisols retiennent davantage la chlordécone.
2. L'historique bananier est un prédicteur majeur de contamination.
3. Les zones de forte pluviométrie amplifient la contamination.
4. Le clustering révèle des profils territoriaux distincts.
5. Prioriser la surveillance sur les communes critiques identifiées.
6. Adapter les pratiques agricoles selon le type de sol.
")
## 
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════
## RECOMMANDATIONS — AIDE À LA DÉCISION
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════
## 
## 1. Les Andosols et Nitisols retiennent davantage la chlordécone.
## 2. L'historique bananier est un prédicteur majeur de contamination.
## 3. Les zones de forte pluviométrie amplifient la contamination.
## 4. Le clustering révèle des profils territoriaux distincts.
## 5. Prioriser la surveillance sur les communes critiques identifiées.
## 6. Adapter les pratiques agricoles selon le type de sol.
